text: **Anna:** Hi there!
**Anna:** In Washington, DC, people do many things in the {evening|夕方/夜|/ˈiːvnɪŋ/|We enjoy evening walks.}
**Anna:** They go listen to {music|音楽|/ˈmjuːzɪk/|I love classical music.}
**Anna:** They eat at a {restaurant|レストラン|/ˈrestrɒnt/|We dined at a nice restaurant.}
**Anna:** They go to the {theater|劇場/映画館|/ˈθɪətər/|We watched a play at the theater.}
**Anna:** Tonight, I am going to the theater with my friends.
**Anna:** But I don't know what {clothes|衣服|/kloʊðz/|These clothes are stylish.} to wear.
**Anna:** Maybe this {magazine|雑誌|/ˈmæɡəziːn/|I read a fashion magazine.} can help.
**Anna:** Her clothes are {beautiful|美しい|/ˈbjuːtɪfəl/|She wore a beautiful dress.}
**Anna:** I really want a friend like her to help me.
**Anna:** Who are you?
**Anna:** I am Jeannie.
**Genie:** You want help?
**Genie:** I am here to help you find the right {clothes|衣服|/kloʊðz/|Choose the right clothes.}
**Anna:** Awesome!
**Anna:** How about {jeans|ジーンズ|/dʒiːnz/|I wear blue jeans.} and a t-shirt?
**Genie:** No!
**Genie:** Jeans and a t-shirt are too {casual|カジュアルな|/ˈkæʒuəl/|Casual clothes are comfortable.}
**Anna:** How about something more {formal|フォーマルな|/ˈfɔːrməl/|Wear formal attire.}?
**Anna:** Sure.
**Anna:** Wow, Jeannie, this {dress|ドレス|/dres/|She bought a new dress.} is beautiful, but it's not the right size.
**Anna:** It's too small.
**Anna:** Yes, it is too small.
**Genie:** But {green|緑色の|/ɡriːn/|Green is my favorite color.} looks great on you.
**Anna:** Thanks.
**Genie:** Take off the green dress
**Genie:** Let's try a green shirt and a {skirt|スカート|/skɜːrt/|She wore a long skirt.}
**Anna:** Oh, Jeannie, this green shirt is too large
**Anna:** And this {orange|オレンジ色の|/ˈɔːrɪndʒ/|Orange is a bright color.} skirt is too orange.
**Anna:** Yes, the right size for you is {medium|中間の|/ˈmiːdiəm/|Medium size fits perfectly.}
**Anna:** Let's try {again|もう一度|/əˈɡeɪn/|Let's try again tomorrow.}
**Anna:** Oh uh uh I don't like this {outfit|服装/装い|/ˈaʊtfɪt/|This outfit is too formal.}
**Genie:** No, that does not match {nothing|何も|/ˈnʌθɪŋ/|I found nothing in the box.}

**Anna:** These clothes are {formal|正式な/フォーマルな|/ˈfɔːrməl/|Formal attire is required.}, a {suit|スーツ|/suːt/|He wore a black suit.} jacket, a {dress|ドレス|/dres/|She wore a beautiful dress.} shirt, and a tie.
**Anna:** They look {great|素晴らしい|/ɡreɪt/|That's a great idea!}
**Genie:** Those clothes look great.
**Anna:** For a man, something is {wrong|間違っている|/rɔːŋ/|Something is wrong with the car.}
**Anna:** Let me see.
**Anna:** There, now try.
**Genie:** Oh thanks.
**Genie:** Now these clothes look great on you.
**Anna:** They do
**Anna:** Um, Jeannie, can you put on a {gold|金の|/ɡoʊld/|A gold ring sparkled.} belt?
**Anna:** Sure.
**Genie:** That looks great.
**Anna:** Can you put on a jacket?
**Genie:** Why not?
**Anna:** I love the jacket.
**Anna:** How about a hat?
**Genie:** Why not?
**Genie:** Mmm, take off the hat.
**Genie:** That's better.
**Anna:** Jeannie, these clothes look and feel {great|素晴らしい|/ɡreɪt/|That's a great performance!}
**Anna:** Let's go to the {theater|劇場|/ˈθɪətər/|We went to the theater last night.}
**Genie:** Sorry, Anna.
**Genie:** I have to help other friends.
**Genie:** Go to the {magazine|雑誌|/ˈmæɡəziːn/|I read a fashion magazine.} if you want me to help again.
**Anna:** Thanks, Jeannie.
**Anna:** Sure thing.
**Anna:** Goodbye.

**Anna:** There are many {places|場所/スポット|/ˈpleɪsɪz/|We visited many beautiful places.} in {DC|首都ワシントン|/diː.siː/|DC is the capital of the United States.} to go for a {great|素晴らしい/素晴らしな|/ɡreɪt/|It was a great day.} evening out.
**Anna:** And it's nice to have a {friend|友人/友達|/frend/|My friend helped me today.} to help me look my {best|最高の/最良の|/best/|You look your best today.}
**Anna:** Until next time.
**Anna:** Bye!
